title: carduus pycnocephalus subsp. cinereus
intro: Carduus pycnocephalus subsp. cinereus, also known as the Grey-headed Thistle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its prickly stems and purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Carduus pycnocephalus subsp. cinereus include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as promoting digestion and immune system function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to support overall health and well-being.
